steps.text: Determine if the licensee’s procedures for radiation protection at the facility meet regulatory requirements, and licensee commitments.
Determine if Form NRC-3, "Notice to Employees," is posted as required.
Determine if restricted areas are posted and controlled as required.
Determine if appropriate protective clothing is used by facility personnel as required.
Determine if the licensee’s personnel dosimetry program is effective, maintained and meets regulatory requirements and licensee commitments.
Determine if licensee personnel, equipment and materials exiting a controlled area are monitored as required.
Determine if personnel working with or around radioactive materials have been instructed in radiation protection as required.
Determine if required radiation surveys, sampling and monitoring were performed in accordance with regulatory requirements and licensee procedures.
Determine if the licensee has implemented a calibration program for the survey, sampling and monitoring instruments in use as required.
Determine if exposure limits in 10 CFR Part 20 were satisfied.
Use direct observation to verify inspection requirements.
Use some combination of records review, independent measurements, or discussions or walk-throughs with involved licensee personnel if direct observation is not used.
Determine if a potential or actual hazard exists.
Consult with responsible management and the NRR project manager.
Initiate appropriate regulatory action.

preconditions.text: Inspection requirements are not already fulfilled in other inspection procedures.
The inspector has consulted with responsible management and the NRR project manager.

constraints.text: Do not use reference guidance as requirements unless the licensee has committed to the specific guidance document in writing.

steps.text: Review changes to the facility's radiation protection procedures.
Verify Form NRC-3 is conspicuously posted.
Ask the licensee where Form NRC-3 forms are posted and directly observe the postings in one or two such areas.
Observe the controls for two radiologically controlled areas.

preconditions.text: Postings of Form NRC-3 are not readily apparent.

steps.text: Evaluate the licensee’s use of controls for radiation areas unique to Research and Test reactors, such as pneumatic irradiation sample transfer systems for 'rabbits,' beam ports, and other potential radiation work areas for experimental programs.
Evaluate the licensee’s use of shielding and controls used to remove or modify shielding.

constraints.text: Do not enter a high radiation area without a licensee escort trained in radiation protection who is knowledgeable of the high radiation area and the health physics precautions to be taken.

steps.text: Check alarming dosimeters (chirpers) before each operation to assure that they are operational.
Assess the need for extremity monitors for handling activated samples, and activated or contaminated experimental equipment.
Observe two selected facility workers leaving the applicable controlled area to determine if the dosimeter readings are actually recorded as required.
Review the process of film or TLD badge processing.

preconditions.text: If licensee procedures require that personnel exposures on direct reading dosimeters be recorded
If the licensee processes its own film or TLD badges

constraints.text: Dosimetry must be worn as required.
Dosimetry processing must meet the requirements of 10 CFR 20.1501 for the National Voluntary Laboratory Accreditation Program accreditation.

steps.text: Review the dosimetry process, including TLD badges.
Observe four facility workers leaving a controlled area as a sample for the exit survey inspection.
Perform an appropriate check of all personnel, tools, equipment, and protective clothing for contamination before leaving the controlled area.
Detect alpha contamination using a hand held gas flow proportional counter with a thin mylar window or by smear survey and analyses using a 2 or 4 pi gas flow proportional counter.
Perform nasal smears.

preconditions.text: If hand held friskers are used, hands must be monitored before the probe is removed from its mounting.
Personnel contamination by alpha emitters is suspected.

constraints.text: Dosimetry must meet the requirements of 10 CFR 20.1501 for the National Voluntary Laboratory Accreditation Program accreditation.
The exit monitor must be shielded from extraneous radiation sources that would mask contamination.

steps.text: Evaluate the radiation safety training received by two permanent facility workers and, where applicable, two experimenters or students.
Observe respective activities of the selected individuals.
Discuss the radiation safety training received by these individuals to confirm that appropriate training was actually received and understood.
Confirm whether instruction appropriate to the tasks to be performed was provided to these individuals and if there was adequate supervision of their activities.
Observe two radiological surveys, sampling, or monitoring activities, other than exit surveys.

preconditions.text: Experimenters or students are applicable to the facility sample.

constraints.text: It is important that experimenters and students understand the radiation protection requirements associated with their assigned activities.

steps.text: Observe two radiological surveys, sampling, or monitoring activities (other than exit surveys).
Accompany a person qualified in the licensee's radiation control techniques on normal rounds, or request to be shown how tasks are normally accomplished.
Determine by direct observation and discussion whether personnel conducting these activities are qualified by training and experience.
Verify whether technicians take smears or instrument readings, as appropriate, in areas readily accessible to facility personnel.
Focus attention on beam ports, experimental stations, and ion exchangers for cleanup of primary water, where applicable.
Examine the licensee's training records to confirm that technicians are sufficiently trained.
Verify if surveys were performed as required by the licensee's procedures.
Verify if the surveys indicate that exposure rates in these areas are consistent with the licensee's ALARA program.

preconditions.text: Operability checks (e.g., source and battery checks) must be performed on all instrumentation before use.
Concerns arise regarding whether technicians are sufficiently trained.

constraints.text: All instrumentation used during these activities are required to be operable, calibrated, and maintained as required by 10 CFR 20.1501(b) and the licensee’s procedures.

steps.text: Verify if there is reasonable agreement with the licensee's records for survey, sampling, and monitoring.
Determine how the licensee assures that airborne contamination levels are within the regulatory requirements of 10 CFR Part 20, Subparts C and D.
Examine surveys of solid waste material to determine whether the licensee complies with their procedures and requirements.
Verify the calibration of radiation monitoring instrument(s) used in the licensee's radiation surveys.
Verify if all survey, sampling, and monitoring instruments have current calibrations appropriate for the types and energies of radiation to be detected.
Examine the technical adequacy of calibration procedures.

preconditions.text: The potential exists for airborne contamination
The facility performs its own calibrations

constraints.text: Records for survey, sampling and monitoring are required to be maintained in accordance with 10 CFR 20.2103.
Information Notice 85-92, 'Surveys of Wastes Before Disposal from Nuclear Reactor Facilities,' is to be used as technical guidance.
Requirements for the calibration of survey, sampling and monitoring instruments can be found in 10 CFR 20.1501(b).
ANSI N323, 'Radiation Protection Instrumentation Test and Calibration,' 1978, provides additional guidance in this area.

steps.text: Examine the technical adequacy of calibration procedures at facilities that perform their own calibrations.
Review 50 percent of the facility's personnel dosimetry record.
Review the issue further to determine that the radiation exposure is consistent with the licensee's ALARA program.

Determine if the licensee reported the overexposures in accordance with 10 CFR Parts 20.2202 and 20.2203, how the licensee addressed the problem, and inform appropriate management and the responsible NRR project manager.
Review major changes in organization, personnel, facilities, equipment, programs, and procedures that could affect radiation protection activities since the last inspection.
Review any organizational change involving the radiation protection manager.
Give particular attention to the reporting chain above the facility director if required by the TS.

preconditions.text: If individuals routinely exceed 20 percent of the exposure limits
If there were exposures greater than the 10 CFR Part 20 limits

constraints.text: The licensee is required to keep exposure records (e.g., TLD or film badge records) on form NRC-5 or equivalent (See 10 CFR 20.2106).
Changes in the facility or procedures described in the safety analysis report are required to be made in accordance with 10 CFR 50.59.

steps.text: Be aware of and understand the nature and cause of the events and the corrective actions taken.
Inform appropriate management and the NRR project manager if radiologically significant events were not effectively addressed by the licensee.
Verify ALARA implementation for one routinely performed experiment or process having an actual or potential for a relatively high radiological exposure.
Determine the effectiveness of the licensee's ALARA program by conducting discussions with operations or radiation protection personnel.
Verify if facility supervisors periodically perform observations of staff effectiveness in radiological work practices, work monitoring, procedural compliance, and survey adequacy.
Verify if the radiation protection program is periodically reviewed and audits are performed by the radiation safety group.

preconditions.text: The ALARA part of the inspection has not been covered under other inspection requirements, or has not previously received adequate evaluation.

constraints.text: Refer to 10 CFR 20.1101(b) and the licensee's administrative controls during ALARA evaluation.

steps.text: Verify if the radiation protection program is periodically reviewed as required by 10 CFR 20.1101(c) and if audits are performed by the radiation safety group in accordance with their program requirements.
Verify if the safety committee audits and evaluates the radiation protection program to assure compliance with 10 CFR Part 20, the TSs, and other program requirements.
Review the Respiratory Protection Program to ensure the use of respiratory protection equipment and other controls to limit intakes of radioactive material consistent with ALARA.
Review compliance and records for planned special exposures in accordance with 10 CFR requirements.

preconditions.text: If planned special exposures are conducted or expected at the non-power reactor.

constraints.text: Declaration of pregnancy must be voluntary and must be in writing.
The declaration is revocable by the woman and the woman does not need to provide any 'medical proof' of pregnancy.
